title: Lyciodes parvifolium
taxonomy: Lyciodes parvifolium is a species of flowering plant in the family Caprifoliaceae. It is native to China and belongs to the genus Lyciodes, which is part of the Caprifoliaceae family. The plant's classification places it within the kingdom Plantae, with further classification within the subkingdom Angiosperm, class Magnoliopsida, and order Dipsacales. Within the family Caprifoliaceae, Lyciodes parvifolium is a member of the tribe Diervillieae. The plant's specific classification is still a subject of ongoing research and may be subject to revision in the future.
uses: Lyciodes parvifolium, also known as the small-leaved twinflower, has been found to have potential medicinal properties. Its leaves and flowers contain bioactive compounds, which may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Traditionally, the plant has been used in some cultures to treat various health issues, including fever and digestive problems.
